ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_03411433 represents raw chicken meat broadly; the specific 'whole, without skin' preparation is not individually coded. Closest defensible match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC covers raw chicken meat; the skinless whole-bird variant is not separately coded. Closest defensible FoodEx2 anchor used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 226955001 represents chicken as a food substance; raw skinless preparation is not individually coded.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical raw serving of skinless whole chicken is approximately 100 g, representing a single portion before cooking, equivalent to a medium chicken breast half or two drumsticks without skin.

nutrient_source.title: Chicken, broilers or fryers, meat only, raw — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB 05011); cross-referenced with Singapore HPB Foodo database entry for Chicken, whole, raw, without skin (F11010036)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service / Health Promotion Board Singapore
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Primary nutrient values sourced from Singapore HPB Foodo (F11010036, confidence 0.95). Cross-validated against USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for raw skinless chicken meat (NDB 05011 / FDC ID 171477). Values are consistent across both sources. HPB values used as primary given Singapore provenance.

gi_source.title: N/A — Glycemic index not applicable for negligible-carbohydrate protein food
gi_source.source_match_notes: Raw skinless chicken contains 0 g carbohydrate per 100 g. Glycemic index is not applicable as this food contains negligible carbohydrates. No GI value is assigned. Glycemic load per serving is 0.0 by definition.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium (77 mg/100 g) with meaningful potassium (229 mg/100 g) supporting a favourable Na:K ratio aligned with DASH dietary principles
hypertension.guidance: Raw skinless chicken is an excellent protein choice for hypertension management; prepare by grilling, baking, or steaming without added salt, and avoid high-sodium marinades such as soy sauce or oyster sauce to preserve the naturally low sodium profile.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Zero carbohydrate and zero sugar content with high protein (21.4 g/100 g) supporting satiety and glycaemic stability without raising postprandial blood glucose
type2_diabetes.guidance: Raw skinless chicken has no glycaemic impact and is an ideal protein source for type 2 diabetes; choose low-fat cooking methods such as grilling or baking, avoid sugar-based marinades or batters, and pair with non-starchy vegetables to create a balanced, glucose-friendly meal.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low total fat (3.08 g/100 g) and low saturated fat (0.86 g/100 g) with moderate cholesterol (70 mg/100 g) and high lean protein supporting heart-healthy dietary patterns
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Raw skinless chicken is a heart-healthy lean protein; removing the skin substantially reduces saturated fat intake, and preparation by grilling or baking without added saturated fats keeps the cardiovascular risk profile low in line with AHA dietary guidelines.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Absence of omega-3 fatty acids, antioxidants, polyphenols, and dietary fibre means no active anti-inflammatory benefit despite low saturated fat
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Skinless raw chicken is not pro-inflammatory but provides no active anti-inflammatory benefit for rheumatoid arthritis; pair with anti-inflammatory ingredients such as turmeric, ginger, or extra-virgin olive oil during preparation, and serve with omega-3-rich sides or antioxidant-rich vegetables to create an RA-supportive meal.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Chicken, whole, raw, without skin is a raw, uncooked whole chicken from which the outer skin has been removed prior to preparation. The exposed meat displays a pale pink colour with a firm, slightly moist texture across all cuts including breast, thigh, drumstick, and wing portions. Removing the skin eliminates the majority of the fat layer, resulting in a very lean protein source with only 3.08 g total fat and 0.86 g saturated fat per 100 g. The food provides 119 kcal and 21.39 g of high-quality complete protein per 100 g, making it one of the most efficient lean protein sources available. Carbohydrate content is zero, sugar is zero, and dietary fibre is zero, reflecting the pure animal-protein nature of the food. Sodium is naturally low at 77 mg per 100 g, and potassium at 229 mg per 100 g supports a favourable sodium-to-potassium ratio. Cholesterol is moderate at 70 mg per 100 g. The food is rich in B vitamins including niacin (9.91 mg), vitamin B6 (0.63 mg), and vitamin B12 (0.31 mcg), and provides meaningful amounts of phosphorus (182 mg), zinc (1.31 mg), and selenium (13.9 mcg). Clinically, this food is highly suitable for type 2 diabetes management due to zero carbohydrate content, for hypertension due to low sodium and adequate potassium, and for cardiovascular disease prevention due to low saturated fat. It is halal-certified, gluten-free, and dairy-free. It is widely used as a base ingredient across global cuisines and is suitable for grilling, baking, steaming, poaching, stir-frying, and braising. The glycemic index is not applicable as the food contains negligible carbohydrates.

confidence_reason: Primary nutrient values sourced directly from Singapore HPB Foodo database (F11010036) with confidence 0.95, cross-validated against USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy for raw skinless chicken meat. Both sources are approved primary sources and values are consistent. The food is a well-characterised generic raw ingredient with stable nutrient composition.
notes: Carbohydrate_g is naturally zero as raw chicken meat contains no carbohydrates. Sugar_g is naturally zero as raw chicken meat contains no sugars. Dietary_fibre_g is naturally zero as animal muscle tissue contains no dietary fibre. Confirmed zero per Singapore HPB Foodo (F11010036) and USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 171477). Saturated_fat_g (0.86 g), monounsaturated_fat_g (1.24 g), polyunsaturated_fat_g (0.66 g), trans_fat_g (0.02 g), and detailed B-vitamin and mineral values sourced from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 171477) for raw skinless chicken meat as these fields are not individually reported in the HPB source. Vitamin_c_mg set to 0.0 (trace, confirmed negligible per USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy FDC ID 171477 — reported as 0 mg for raw skinless chicken meat). Vitamin_a_mcg_rae set to 9.0 mcg RAE per USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 171477). Folate_mcg_dfe set to 4.0 mcg DFE per USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 171477). Vitamin_d_mcg set to 0.1 mcg per USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 171477). Vitamin_e_mg set to 0.27 mg per USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 171477). All previously sentinel-valued micronutrients have been resolved to numeric values from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 171477) to satisfy G3 numeric hygiene requirements. Glycemic index is not applicable as this food contains negligible carbohydrates (0 g per 100 g); gi_category set to 'not_applicable' and glycemic_index set to null per the low/zero-carbohydrate food rule. This record is a generic raw ingredient record; nutrient values will vary depending on the specific cut (breast vs thigh vs drumstick) and individual bird characteristics. The HPB source represents a composite whole-bird average without skin.

q4_glycemic_completeness.notes: glycemic_index set to null (not 0) as food contains negligible carbohydrates per the critical q4 rule for low/zero-carbohydrate foods. gi_category set to 'not_applicable'. glycemic_load_per_serving set to 0.0. gi_source fully populated with explanation.
